analysis_result: 4/54 (7.4%) SUZ12 mutations from MOESM5 WES MAF
discrepancy_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED: MOESM5 Supplementary Table 2 MAF (7457 rows x 123 cols, 52 unique Tumor_Sample_Barcodes - MAF denominator vs 54 claimed in Fig 2b caption). Fig 2b oncoprint as displayed in paper PDF page 4.

B. COHORT DEFINITION: WES cohort (n=54) per Fig 2b caption 'Results from whole exome sequencing (n = 54) with frequency and mutations identified'. Comprises MPNSTs, atypical neurofibromas, and neurofibromas (Tumor_Type legend).

C. VARIABLE CODING: Outcome variable = SUZ12 alteration. Model counted only SUZ12 rows in MAF (Hugo_Symbol == 'SUZ12'): 4 samples (Tumor_M2252, Tumor_M2372, Tumor_M3048, Tumor_RT6712) with Frame_Shift_Del (n=2) and Nonsense_Mutation (n=2) variant classifications. Fig 2b oncoprint Alterations legend: Deletion (red), Indel (black), Gain (blue), Truncating (green), Missense (orange) -- so the oncoprint includes CNV deletions and gains in addition to point mutations/indels.

D. COMPUTATION: Model result: 4 unique samples with SUZ12 mutation / 54 WES cohort = 7.4%. Reported oncoprint result: 11% => 0.11 x 54 = 5.94 => 6 samples with SUZ12 alterations (mutations + CNV deletions).

E. RESULT: Model 7.4% vs reported 11%. Relative difference = (11-7.4)/11 = 32.7% -- exceeds +-10% concordance threshold => DISCREPANT.

F. ROOT CAUSE: The Fig 2b oncoprint aggregates multiple alteration types. The MAF in MOESM5 contains only somatic short variants (SNVs/indels). Arm-level and focal copy-number loss data are not in MOESM5. Cross-validation: CDKN2A appears 58% in oncoprint yet 0 samples in MAF -- confirms oncoprint includes CNV deletions as the primary mechanism. Similarly EED is 13% in oncoprint but only 1 sample has EED mutation in MAF. For SUZ12, an additional ~2 samples likely have SUZ12 deletion (not mutation), bringing total to 6/54 = 11%.

G. PROPOSED FIX: Integrate SUZ12 somatic mutations from MOESM5 MAF with SUZ12 copy-number deletions from the study's CNV calls (derived from methylation CNV via conumee or from WES-based CNV calling -- not included in MOESM5). Combined altered samples should equal 6/54 = 11.1%.
proposed_fix: Include SUZ12 copy-number loss (Deletion, per Fig 2b legend) in addition to short-variant mutations from MOESM5 MAF. The oncoprint integrates mutations + CNV events; expected result is 6/54 = 11.1% when both alteration types are counted.
